characters.description: A defeated bandit leader from the earlier conflict involving Fa Ram. His ambush killed people under Cai Xiulan's command, leaving her burdened by their deaths and by the public account of his defeat.
characters.name: Sun Ken

characters.description: The farmer and head of Fa Ram, newly married to Meiling. Jin is enlarging the farm, sharing its work among a growing household, and teaching Cai Xiulan while continuing to value useful labor over status.
characters.name: Jin

characters.description: Jin's wife, a healer and farmer who shares responsibility for Fa Ram's home, fields, and expanding household. Three weeks into their marriage, she works beside Jin and helps direct the farm's daily routines.
characters.name: Meiling

characters.description: A cultivator of the Verdant Blade Sect who has come to Fa Ram to learn Jin's farming methods. She eagerly takes on work in the western field under his direction.
characters.name: Cai Xiulan

characters.description: Jin's friend from Hong Yaowu and a dependable hand at Fa Ram. He helps expand the farm and uses chi to reinforce the rice-paddy walls.
characters.name: Gou Ren

characters.description: Jin's ox and plowing partner. Although cultivation has made Jin capable of doing more of the physical work himself, he continues to give Babe a valued place in the farm's labor.
characters.name: Babe

characters.description: A chi-conducting plow made from a former demonic blade. Jin names it Sunny while using it with Babe to prepare a root-vegetable field.
characters.name: Sunny

characters.description: Jin's first disciple and Fa Ram's senior guardian. An awakened rooster entering his second year of awareness, Bi De protects the farm, carries Ri Zu, and treats Jin's instructions as solemn duties.
characters.name: Bi De

characters.description: Meiling's small medicine student and a disciple of Fa Ram. Ri Zu rides on Bi De's back and already has substantial knowledge of herbs, poisons, and the importance of dosage.
characters.name: Ri Zu

characters.description: An awakened cat of Fa Ram and a swift fighter who uses chi-enhanced claws. Her challenge to Cai Xiulan begins in hostility, but their contests soon establish them as training partners.
characters.name: Tigu

characters.description: A boar disciple of Fa Ram and Pi Pa's partner. He helps the household watch over its pregnant cattle and takes part in the farm's shared work and protection.
characters.name: Chun Ke

characters.description: A sow disciple of Fa Ram and Chun Ke's partner. She helps guard the farm's livestock and is part of the coordinated animal household supporting Jin's growing farm.
characters.name: Pi Pa

characters.description: Fa Ram's carp disciple. He joins Bi De, Ri Zu, Chun Ke, and Pi Pa when Jin asks the household to search for a queen bee, treating the request as a direct duty.
characters.name: Wa Shi

characters.description: An injured solitary queen bee found by Bi De and Ri Zu. They bring her safely to Fa Ram, where Jin places her in a large hive.
characters.name: Injured queen bee

characters.description: A damaged land spirit linked to Jin's chi and the ground of Fa Ram. Cai Xiulan perceives a childlike figure marked by golden cracks, but her forceful approach is rejected before she can understand it.
characters.name: Fa Ram earth spirit

characters.description: A young man of Hong Yaowu experimenting with ways to capture lasting images. His first attempts fail without the costly recording crystal he believes the technique requires.
characters.name: Yun Ren

characters.description: Meiling's younger brother, a child of Hong Yaowu who misses her and is reluctant to continue his medical-history studies. He encounters Yun Ren while the young man is attempting to capture a lasting image.
characters.name: Meiling's younger brother

characters.description: Meiling's father and an elder of Hong Yaowu. He receives Meiling, Jin, Cai Xiulan, and Gou Ren warmly when they arrive in the village after the spring planting.
characters.name: Hong Xian

characters.description: A giant black turtle tending a garden in the hidden watery realm reached by Wa Shi. The turtle identifies him as a young dragon and offers to teach him water manipulation.
characters.name: Unnamed black turtle

characters.description: A senior disciple of the Cloudy Sword Sect assigned to trace Jin Jue and deliver correspondence. Courteous and discreet, he follows the former disciple's path toward the Azure Hills before being recalled.
characters.name: Lu Ri

characters.description: Meiling's close friend in Verdant Hill. Meiling asks to stay in town and support her while she awaits childbirth.
characters.name: Meihua

characters.description: Verdant Hill’s lord magistrate. He authorizes Jin’s proposed road to Hong Yaowu and asks that Bi De address dangerous animals troubling the area.
characters.name: Lord magistrate of Verdant Hill

characters.description: The magistrate's wife in Verdant Hill. She helps Meiling identify the source of the town's contaminated water and offers practical instruction in negotiation and elite society after Meiling treats her long-standing pain and tremors.
characters.name: Lady of Verdant Hill

characters.description: Meihua's husband and the father of her newborn son. He receives confirmation that both mother and child are healthy and begins considering the boy's eventual formal name.
characters.name: Ting Feng

characters.description: A young defender of Eighth Correct Place who faces the red-eyed wolf leading the siege of his village. He wounds the creature before Bi De arrives, then asks the rooster to teach him.
characters.name: Zheng Fei

characters.description: The unnatural red-eyed wolf leading the pack that besieges Eighth Correct Place. It kills villagers and breaches the settlement before Bi De beheads it.
characters.name: Terror

characters.description: An awakened queen who survived the destruction of her former swarm and reached Fa Ram near the loss of reason. Jin's care restores her awareness, and ordinary hives accept her authority as she begins rebuilding under the farm's protection.
characters.name: Bee queen

characters.description: A white rabbit tending the graves of a village destroyed by Sun Ken. She appears as an awakened guardian and confronts Bi De when he enters the ruined settlement.
characters.name: White rabbit

characters.description: An awakened white rabbit who tends the graves of a village destroyed by Sun Ken. She is Miantiao's devoted disciple and has been trained as a fighter for the purpose of killing a demon.
characters.name: Yin

characters.description: Yin's snake master, a scarred survivor living amid the remains of a ruined village. He is missing an eye, badly disabled, and claims that he and Yin mean to rebuild the settlement.
characters.name: Miantiao

characters.description: The sentient identity of the plow used at Fa Ram. With its third wielder, it learns to cut soil while turning aside most roots, bringing its urge to cut under greater control.
characters.name: Sonny

characters.description: A body-stealing infiltrator who killed Zang Li and now occupies the young master's body. His tournament victory restores his standing in the Shrouded Mountain Sect, allowing him to continue his predation behind a respected identity.
characters.name: Lu Ban

characters.description: A giant white fox who guards a hidden tomb, its residence, and the spirit sword Summer's Sky. The guardian spares Yun Ren because he respected the grave and agrees to teach him a way to preserve images.
characters.name: White fox guardian

characters.description: The name Bi De chooses after returning from his long investigation. He declines Jin's family name and instead identifies himself with Fa Ram, the home whose mysteries and safety continue to guide his work.
characters.name: Fa Bi De
